meaning: vt.使暴露于空气中,使充满气体
examples: Aerate your lawn to allow it to breathe and absorb water better.
The potatoes were set outside to aerate.
We aerate the soil allowing oxygen to be present in small amounts through our anthills.
We still have the time and resources required to fix our economic plumbing, de-aerate our money, and make a smooth and orderly recovery.
